title: Upon His Kinswoman, Mistress Bridget Herrick.
content:
Sweet Bridget blush'd, and therewithal
    Fresh blossoms from her cheeks did fall.
    I thought at first 'twas but a dream,
    Till after I had handled them
    And smelt them, then they smelt to me
    As blossoms of the almond tree.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "Upon His Kinswoman, Mistress Bridget Herrick.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
